posts.alt_text: 7 Ultimate Weekly Pool Maintenance Checklist
posts.excerpt: Swimming pool maintenance requires consistent attention and proper care. This checklist covers seven essential weekly maintenance steps to keep your pool safe, clean, and functioning properly.
posts.body:
The Ultimate Weekly Pool Maintenance Checklist for Pool Owners

Swimming pool maintenance is one of the factors that you should keep constant with the checklist being given priority.

Pool checks continuously will enable you to have good control of your swimming base. In case of an algae outbreak, you can easily locate the defective area.

Damage like leakages, broken tiles and filter blockages are problems that arise due to a lack of proper pool maintenance.

Human injuries can also arise due to these factors. You should ensure that it's properly checked before using the swimming pool to avoid these uncertainties.

Proper pool maintenance will ensure that everyone gets comfortable accessing it. The checklists act as safety measures and guides.

The following steps will help you during the weekly maintenance of your pool.

7 things to consider when maintaining your swimming pool

  1. Keep the deck clean and free of debris.
  2. Shock your pool.
  3. Check the water level of your pool.
  4. Check the ladder tension.
  5. Replace the cartridge elements.
  6. Check on the filtration system.
  7. Check the baskets for leaves.

Step No. 1: Swab the deck.

When looking for a pool, it's great to go for the clean one. You should maintain it by scrubbing and washing around the area. You must clean and get rid of the debris or mud that may end up in the pool. This avoids situations such as contamination and cloudy water.

Moreover, proper maintenance of a swimming pool increases the chances of people coming over to your pool, if you are using your pool for commercial purposes.

Step No. 2: Shock your pool.

Shocking the pool means adding chemicals to destroy any bacteria in the pool.

This step is pretty good because it reduces the chances of contamination. You should do it every week depending on the amount of use your pool gets. Especially during summer, you may need to shock more than once per week.

It is prudent to shock your pool after occasions such as parties or heavy rainfalls. This way, you get rid of contaminants in the water.

The amount of the chemicals you are supposed to use depends on the water PH. This is because it may lead to infection in the skin and then cause itchiness or irritation if you use too much.

Step No. 3: Check the water level of your pool.

The water level is another factor you should check when maintaining your pool. During heavy rainfall, the water level of the pool may increase gradually.

You will have to pump it out to avoid overflowing the pool water when this occurs. A lot of water in the pool will flow out and even lead to the breakage of terminals.

There is always a mark on the wall line when constructing a pool. This denotes the optimal water level that should be met.

The inground pools water levels should be half the skimmer plate. This will prevent the drowning of children when they use the swimming pool.

Step No. 4: Check the ladder tension and swimming safety equipment.

These safety measures are of the most importance. When maintaining a pool, checking the bolts if they are tightened is a must step.

The diving board bolts or the ladders get loose at times due to people jumping and sliding on them. Tightening the bolts will ensure a safe environment so no one is injured.

With loose bolts, the higher the risks of falling or the occurrence of injuries. Make sure to keep safety equipment available in case of emergency.

Step No. 5: Replace the filter elements.

The filters need to be checked regularly. Having new filter cartridges ensures the filters trap dirt from contaminating your pool.

Faulty cartridges lead to blockage of the filters and eventually cause an overflow, or overworking the pump. They will not be able to work well forever, so check them regularly.

Lubricating the O-rings and tuning the pool cleaners will ensure the pool runs smoothly without any default.

Aside from routine cleaning of the filter elements, check whether they need replacement. Always have a spare, so you are not caught off-guard.

Step No. 6: Check the filtration system.

The filtration system determines how the circulation of the swimming pool water works. It also keeps the water crystalline and transparent by retaining dirt.

The filtration system should be closely monitored to ensure no blockage. Pool filters eventually stop working if they are full of dirt. Your pool should circulate clean water at all times.

The system should be able to clarify the water which has suspended particles. It simply passes it through a porous material and comes out clean.

It ensures that there is no proliferation of materials like algae. The closed-circuit circulation should be guarded and maintained closely.

It is recommended that you filter the pool water at least 8 hours a day during the summer for the filter to work effectively. Less time might be appropriate during winter.

Step No. 7: Check the baskets.

You should keep checking the pump baskets every week. It has a clear lid so you can easily identify if it is full.

The skimmer baskets should be emptied so that the suction part remains intact, effective, and strong. Make sure to check more often if you have a strong wind storm that blows extra debris into the pool.

You should also ensure that the basket is basically in the right area before it starts functioning.
posts.description: Swimming pool maintenance requires consistent attention and proper care. This checklist covers seven essential weekly maintenance steps to keep your pool safe, clean, and functioning properly.

posts.title: A Step-by-Step Guide to Clean a Green Swimming Pool
posts.author: Clean Living Pool and Spa
posts.alt_text: A Step-by-Step Guide to Clean a Green Swimming Pool
posts.excerpt: A dirty pool is usually green due to algae growth from low chlorine levels. This guide provides 9 steps to clean a green swimming pool and restore crystalline water.
posts.body:
Owning a swimming pool is easy, but keeping it "swim ready" is a big job. A dirty pool is usually green due to algae growth, which appears due to low chlorine levels.

Other factors that make a pool infested with algae are when it is exposed to high heat, heavy rain, and poor circulation of water. No one wants a dirty pool.

However, don't worry because we will show you how to ensure your pool is always clean with crystalline water at all times.

Here are 9 steps to follow to clean a green swimming pool.

  1. Net out any debris in the pool.
  2. Clean the skimmers and replace the nets.
  3. Brush the walls and floor of the pool.
  4. Clean the inbuilt pump filter.
  5. Backwash the filter.
  6. Test and balance the pool water pH.
  7. Apply shock treatment by adding Chlorine.
  8. Place a flocculant in one of the skimmer baskets.
  9. Backwash the sand filter again.

Step No. 1: Net out any debris in the pool.

Debris in green pool water includes leaves, pollen, twigs, and plastic. Begin the cleaning by removing any floating debris from the pool using a pool skimmer.

A pool skimmer is a net that is attached to the pole. The skimmer will enable you to collect bigger objects that the filter cannot collect.

If your pool has an automatic skimmer, ensure you check it to ascertain that it collects any floating debris.

Step No. 2: Clean the skimmers and replace the nets.

A green pool is a sign that your pool has not been used for a long time, which means a lot of debris will be collected by the skimmer.

The second step is to clean the skimmers and be sure to replace the nets with new ones.

When you clean the skimmer, it will prevent clogging and damage to the filtration system if you are using an automatic skimmer.

Step No. 3: Brush the walls and floor of the pool.

Next, ensure you brush the walls and floor of the pool to remove algae that may have attached to these surfaces.

When you brush the pool's walls and the floor, it will prevent the buildup of algae. This will prevent the algae spores from attaching to the surfaces and growing.

Always remember that it is better to prevent the growth of algae than treat it.

Step No. 4: Clean the inbuilt pump filter

At this point, you must remember to clean the pump filter. When it is clean, it will be able to filter out any debris with ease.

If the pump filter is not clean, the pool water will always be dirty, and eventually, the pump will be damaged.

Step No. 5: Backwash the filter

Backwash the filter depending on the manufacturer's manual to remove any mineral and organic buildup.

Once the filter is clean, the filtration system will work at a maximum flow rate.

Step No. 6: Test and balance the pool water pH.

Test the pH of the pool water as you pay special attention to the chlorine levels. The Chlorine will only work correctly if the pH is balanced.

When Chlorine levels are low, the pool water will turn green. The free Chlorine should be between 1-4ppm. Restore these levels to the right measures if they are off.

When the pH levels are too high, it will cause skin irritation to the swimmers and even corrode the metal parts in the pool.

Be sure to keep the pH levels checked even after cleaning the pool.

Step No. 7: Apply shock treatment by adding chlorine.

Shock treatment is how Chlorine is added to the pool water to raise the levels of free Chlorine.

This is important to prevent the growth of contaminants such as algae, bacteria, and chloramines which make the pool water green.

Depending on the product you are using, follow the instructions indicated and add Chlorine as per the size of your pool.

Step No. 8: Place a flocculant in one of the skimmer baskets.

When the pool is green, the levels of dirt are at the extreme. A flocculant is a chemical added to the pool water when other methods won't clean it up.

Add a flocculant to the skimmer so that it may bind the smaller particles within the pool water to make them larger for ease of collection by the filter.

The particles will sink to the bottom of the pool, and they can also be vacuumed out.

Step No. 9: Backwash the sand filter again.

Your pool water will have cleared up, and it is time to do the final cleaning of the sand filter.

It is important to backwash the sand filter because of the large amounts of dirt collected from the pool.

Be sure to also replace the skimmer nets if they become dirty again. Ensure you have tidied up the pool area once you are done.
posts.description: A dirty pool is usually green due to algae growth from low chlorine levels. This guide provides 9 steps to clean a green swimming pool and restore crystalline water.

posts.title: Easy Tips to Maintain a Perfect Pool in Las Vegas
posts.author: Clean Living Pool and Spa
posts.alt_text: Easy Tips to Maintain a Perfect Pool in Las Vegas
posts.excerpt: A swimming pool in Las Vegas is an amazing amenity, but requires proper maintenance to ensure health and safety. This guide provides seven easy tips for keeping your pool in perfect condition.
posts.body:
A swimming pool in Las Vegas is one of the most amazing amenities you would want to have...especially in a hot environment.

Some of the health risks that may be associated with poor pool maintenance are water borne diseases, physical injuries due to broken tiles, muck and bad aesthetics.

In order to avoid such risks and problems, you should invest in proper pool maintenance. A clean swimming pool ensures overall health of the swimmers and captures their attention.

If you own a pool in Las Vegas, you should have a complete plan to maintain it. With these tips you are certain that your swimming pool will look spick and span.

The following are easy tips that can help you maintain a perfect pool in Las Vegas.

  1. Maintain a weekly pool service routine for your swimming pool.
  2. Ensure there are pool repair services.
  3. Spearhead pool consulting services.
  4. Always test the amounts of Chlorine in the pools
  5. Use test kits to maintain and check the water balance.
  6. Secure the pool properly for winter.
  7. Always power wash your pool deck.

Tip No. 1: Maintain a weekly pool service routine.

A weekly pool service routine can ensure the elimination of debris that floats in the swimming pool. Debris may include leaves, paper, fruit peels, and polythene items.

This skimming process is the cleaning and removal of debris from the pool on a daily basis.

This will eventually eliminate or avoid blockages in the swimming pool, thus affecting the outlet. It also helps in the water mass circulation in and out of the pool.

Brushing the sediments on the pool walls is mandatory to maintain your swimming pool. Through this you will be eliminating awful and unpleasant smells.

When you vacuum the pool, you increase the chances of removing the sediments and algae brushed from the walls from the bottom of the pool.

Tip No. 2: Ensure there are pool repair services.

The swimming pool repair services are among the most important services that need to be taken in. Repairs such as broken tiles need to be fixed to avoid any injuries to swimmers.

Other repair services such as heaters and heat pumps need to be replaced. In Las Vegas, temperatures may sometimes vary, leading to faulty heat pumps in the swimming pools. They regulate the waters so that it may be either warm or cold, depending on your preference. Replacements of filters are used to ensure no harmful substances get into the pool.

Tip No. 3: Spearhead pool consultation services.

Swimming consultation services are a tip that helps you maintain a perfect pool. You can undoubtedly inquire lots of questions on how to maintain your pool from the professionals.

Do not forget that specialists help in opinion advice that may assist in your pool management. It can also lead to new ideas from time to time.

Whether you are looking for pool renovation or construction, their services come in handy.

Tip No. 4: Always test the amount of Chlorine in your pool.

Chlorine is one of the chemicals we use to balance water PH in swimming pools. It is used as a management feature to safely purify and kill bacteria in the water.

You may find some amounts of Chlorine in most swimming pools, but the disadvantage is that it may lead to skin itchiness, dryness, and irritation when used in excess amounts. keeping a close watch on the amounts of chlorine in the water.

Testing the amounts of Chlorine in swimming pools is a way of securing and protecting your loved ones to avoid external uncertainties.

After testing the amounts of Chlorine in the water, be sure to leave it at normal levels. The Chlorine added to the swimming pools is also sometimes used to kill the algae's dominance in the pools.

Whenever the amounts are high, you can heat the swimming pool water till it boils using the heat pumps. Through this, you will be able to reduce the levels of Chlorine.

Tip No. 5: Use test kits to maintain and check water balance.

As the warm weather begins, it's swimming season. This then means that you need to check your water quality. The tests will test four principle indicators.

These tests are what the Las Vegas homeowners need for sure. Through this, you get to ensure that the salt levels (if you have a saltwater pool), phosphates, and Chlorine are at normal levels.

Tip No. 6: Secure the pool properly for winter.

Maintaining a pool during summer is important, but it is more crucial during winter. After all, you do not want to remove your pool cover when winter is over and find the pool full of dangerous bacteria.

When you secure your pool correctly before winter, you will save lots of time and money that would go into repairs in the next season. Be sure to invest in a proper cover to prevent the entry of debris.

Tip No. 7: Always power wash your pool deck.

Aside from maintaining your pool, it is also vital to ensure that the area around your pool is kept clean. Power washing the pool deck removes dust and any weather stains that occur with time.

Begin the process by first sweeping any twigs and leaves out of the pool deck. Remember to overlap the areas you have already cleaned so that no streaks will be seen.

Seek Professional Pool Maintenance Services

Despite having all these tips for maintaining a perfect pool, it might be a daunting task to do it yourself. In some cases, you may make the situation worse than it was.

To avoid any mishap, consult pool maintenance professionals and have an expert examine your pool to give it the perfect look it deserves.

Clean Living Pool and Spa are always ready to help. They specialize in pool maintenance in the Las Vegas area, and they will provide value for your money.

They are experts in acid wash, filter cleaning, tile cleaning, and many more. What are you waiting for? Call them now on(702) 539-3927.
posts.description: A swimming pool in Las Vegas is an amazing amenity, but requires proper maintenance to ensure health and safety. This guide provides seven easy tips for keeping your pool in perfect condition.

posts.title: Easy Steps to Clean Swimming Pool Filters in Las Vegas, NV
posts.author: Clean Living Pool and Spa
posts.alt_text: Easy Steps to Clean Swimming Pool Filters in Las Vegas, NV
posts.excerpt: Pool filters are essential for maintaining healthy, clear water. This guide covers simple steps to clean swimming pool cartridge filters, including manual and chemical cleaning methods.
posts.body:
Just as kidneys are important to your body, so are pool filters to your pool.

Pool filters are essential parts of any pool. Their work is to purify your pool water, maintaining the water healthy, clear, and pure.

According to Forbes, the frequency of cleaning pool filters depends on how often the pool is used.

Some of the indicators that your pool filters need cleaning include a clogged drain and pool odor, or slow water flow.

However, if your pool has a cartridge system, it is recommended to clean it monthly.

If it has a diatomaceous earth (DE) filter, you should disassemble it and clean every three months.

Types of Swimming Pool Filters

There are various types of swimming pool filters and each has unique cleaning requirements.

  1. Sand Filter-Water is purified by passing through a layer of sand. It is known for its low required maintenance and durability.
  2. Cartridge Filter-This is a modern filter that purifies water using a cartridge that is made from synthetic or organic fiber. Although they are expensive, they are the easiest to clean and maintain. Old or damaged cartridges are replaceable.
  3. Diatom Filter-Water is purified by passing through a powder that is made from diatoms. Diatoms are aquatic micro-organisms that are found in oceans and waterways.

In this guide, we will focus on the steps of cleaning pool cartridge filters.

Cartridge filters are the most common filters used in most modern pools.

You can easily know your cartridges are dirty by monitoring the water pressure. When clean, the pressure is high and vice versa.

You can clean your pool's cartridge filters in two ways:

  • Manual cleaning
  • Chemical Cleaning

How to Clean Your Swimming Pool Cartridges Manually

Materials needed: A garden hose, water, air compressor.

Step 1: Turn off the pool system

The first rule of thumb is that you never work on any pool equipment while it's running.

Step 2: Open the filtration system

Filtration systems can be challenging to open. You can read the user manual given when you purchased the system from your provider.

You can either press or use claws to open your pool filtration system. CAUTION: There is a great deal of pressure in water filtration systems. Make sure the pressure has been properly released before you attempt to open the filter housing.

If you're still not sure how to remove it, contact experienced technicians to do the work for you.

Step 3: Remove the Cartridge Filters

Depending on the model of your filtration tank, you can now see up to four filters.

Remove them carefully, one by one.

Set them in an open and dry ground. Ensure that the ground's drainage is perfect and flat.

Step 4: Inspect the filters for damage

Anytime you clean your cartridge filters, make sure you check for cracks in the filter's plastic housing.

Due to wear and tear, it is normal to find cracks in your cartridge filter.

Even the smallest cracks can affect the filters' ability to filter water. If you find cracks in your filter, it is good to replace them with new ones for optimal performance. If the membranes look worn or torn, you should replace them in order to maintain proper filtration.

Step 5: Remove the debris from the filter

After inspecting for damages, it is now time to remove the dirt from your cartridge filter.

The common process of doing this is by applying a water spray on the filter using a garden hose or pressure washer.

Wash off any sticky material you can see on your cartridge filter. Spray the filter from top to down and between the pleats.

P.S- The debris removal process should happen immediately after removing your filters from the tank. If you delay, the debris in your filters will dry, making them harder to remove.

Step 6: Dry the cartridge filters

Once you have removed all the debris from the filters, lay them in the sun to dry. Always wash your filters early on a sunny day.

The sun kills algae and bacteria that might be hidden in your cartridge filter.

Step 7: Remove loose particles from your filter

After confirming that your filter is dry, it is time to use an air compressor to blow off loose debris.

You can also shake your filters while blowing them to remove tiny debris.

After your filter is visually clean, you can return it back to the filtration system.

If the filters look oily after removing them from the sun, consider chemical cleaning.

How to Clean Your Pool Filters Using Chemicals

Chemical cleaning is where you apply chemicals to your cartridges to remove dirt.

In this DIY process, you need the following chemicals to get started:

  • Trisodium Phosphate (TSP) powder. It is one of the common cleaning agents used by several households.
  • Hydrochloric Acid (HCL). It is applied to kill algae and bacteria in the cartridge filters.
  • A sizeable plastic can that can wholly submerge the chemicals and the filters.
  • A nozzle spray
  • Silicon paste to rubricate the filters

Step 1: Read the guides carefully and mix the chemicals in your plastic can.

Step 2: Insert the cartridge filters in the can carefully and wait for an hour or two.

Step 3: Remove the cartridges carefully and set them out to dry.

Step 4: Spray and relubricate the filters

Step 5: Inspect for cracks and return the filters back to the filtration system

Why You Should Hire a Pool Cleaning Company to Clean Your Pool Filters in Las Vegas, NV

A pool is a luxury for lounging with family and friends. But once everyone has left your pool, every dirt, pollen, sweat, and debris is left in your pool.

An optimal filtration system can be of great help, but you need other expenses like buying chemicals, repair, cleaning, and routine maintenance.

Why go through the hassle yet a company can do it at affordable rates?

At Clean Living Pool and Spa, we are a family-owned pool cleaning and maintenance company. We have been servicing North Las Vegas, Summerlin, and Centennial as pool cleaners since 2012. We offer quality pool cleaning and maintenance services to pool owners in Las Vegas, NV.

We also repair and clean pools, clean cartridge filters, and offer consultancy services to pool beginners, step-by-step.
posts.description: Pool filters are essential for maintaining healthy, clear water. This guide covers simple steps to clean swimming pool cartridge filters, including manual and chemical cleaning methods.

posts.title: Pool Maintenance Services vs DIY: A Comparison Guide
posts.author: Clean Living Pool and Spa
posts.alt_text: Pool Maintenance Services vs DIY: A Comparison Guide
posts.excerpt: This guide compares professional pool maintenance services with DIY approaches, examining the differences in cost, quality, and time investment. Pool owners can make an informed decision based on their lifestyle and needs.
posts.body:
Pool Maintenance: DIY vs Professional Services - A Comparison Guide

Maintaining your swimming pool can be time and energy-consuming. It may also cost more than the DIY route, so you need to decide your commitment level.

Unfortunately, according to Mr. Right, failing to maintain your pool on time can be harmful. Plus, it may not be a pleasing task to many pool owners.

Some individuals find cleaning their pool system therapeutic. Others prefer having a great time basking on their poolside and hiring professionals to help maintain it.

But which one is the best approach to keeping your pool maintained and operational? This is a common question asked by many pool owners. Fortunately, this comparison guide will discuss the benefits of hiring pool maintenance services vs those of a DIY approach.

Pool Maintenance Services vs DIY: Overview

Hiring a professional pool maintenance company offers you the best way to care for your swimming pool effectively.

Professionals can help clean your pool and keep it ready for use for a long time. As a result, you'll never worry about your pool having stained liners or dirty water.

Before hiring a skilled pool maintenance team, it's important to understand what exactly they do.

In most cases, their weekly or bi-weekly pool maintenance services will include:

  • Vacuuming and brushing pool surfaces
  • Skimming the pool water
  • Cleaning the pool's deck area and walls
  • Checking pool water pH
  • Monitoring the water level
  • Emptying the pool's skimmer baskets
  • Ensuring effective performance of the filters
  • Maintaining a proper balance of the pool chemicals
  • Shocking the pool

They may also help open your pool during spring and close it at the beginning of winter.

In short, hiring a reliable pool maintenance company makes you the boss. All you do is sit back and handle other essential tasks that require your attention.

You'll also be confident that the pool is clean and safe for you and your family.

Although opting for professional pool maintenance services can be more expensive for some pool owners, many owners believe that the cost is worth it for the peace of mind that it is being done right.

Pool Maintenance Services vs DIY: Comparison

These pool maintenance approaches are similar in that they all have the same goal: to keep a pool system clean, safe, and functional.

But the differences between them are important. Here's how pool maintenance services and DIY approaches differ:

Cost

The cost incurred is one of the key distinguishing factors between hiring a professional and doing the task yourself.

As stated above, a DIY approach can be a cost-efficient way to keep your pool operational. However, you'll still need to invest in good quality pool maintenance equipment such as:

  • Cleaning brush
  • Pool vacuum
  • Pool skimmer

Such equipment can be expensive, but you'll buy them only once.

Chemicals, filter cleaners, and water testing kits require frequent replacements. Therefore, they can be costly over time.

But when you hire an expert to look after your pool, you won't have to buy cleaning products, materials, and equipment.

Instead, you'll pay for the services rendered. The charges may vary from one company to another.

The average cost of cleaning a pool on a one-time depends on the level of algae or contamination present. If you plan to hire the cleaning service once or twice a week, the cost may range from $100 to $150 per month.

Here are the factors that may influence the cost of your professional pool service:

  • Pool size: Larger pools need a lot of chemicals and more time to clean than smaller pools.
  • Location: A pool situated next to trees, foliage, and a wooded area will require regular cleaning more than one in an open space. That's because it collects more debris. Besides, pools located in regions that experience heavy rainfall require regular maintenance.
  • Pool type: The cost of maintaining natural, chlorinated and salted pools vary.
  • Usage: A pool with regular usage will need more frequent maintenance than those used occasionally.
  • Pool equipment: The quality and efficiency of pool equipment will influence performance. Add-on features like lighting and heating will also need more service and repairs.

Quality

A professionally maintained pool will often look better than a DIY project.

That's because the company has trained experts in this industry, meaning they know how to clean your pool properly.

In addition, they use professional-grade equipment and chemicals to clean and keep your pool safe for use. However, the quality may vary depending on the experience of the company you choose.

Consider handling the pool maintenance yourself if you perfectly understand of the following:

  • How a pool functions
  • How to balance and maintain pool chemicals
  • How to clean the pool system
  • How to replace or clean the filters

If you know all these, you can effectively maintain your pool. But if you aren't familiar with them, don't risk it. Instead, hire a professional to help you with the task.

Time and Effort

Pool cleaning and maintenance can be time and effort-consuming. The process involves a lot of tasks like regular cleaning, opening and closing it accordingly, fixing any damage for proper functioning and improved lifespan.

While you may know how to do all these, you may not have enough time or energy to do so more often.

Hiring a professional will relieve you of the burden and stress of maintaining your pool. They use extensive experience and equipment to complete the task in the shortest time possible.

That means you'll have more time to enjoy your pool. A pool maintenance service differs from a DIY project in terms of cost, effort, and quality of the results.

There's no approach that's necessarily better than the other. But it's essential to consider one that suits your lifestyle and needs best.

Contact Pool Maintenance Experts for a Clean and Healthy Pool

You'll always rest assured of exceptional pool maintenance services. Their staff is also fair, dependable, kind, and honest. Feel free to get in touch with them or call them at (702) 539-3927 today!

posts.body:
Questions to Ask Before Hiring a Pool Cleaning Service in Las Vegas

To get the most enjoyment from your swimming pool, you need regular cleaning and maintenance.

You can do the cleaning by yourself. But you may also contact a professional pool cleaning company to do it for you.

According to Bob Villa, a prominent home improvement specialist in the US, pool maintenance requires expert knowledge mainly due to the chemicals required to keep the water in balance.

In this article, we have answered the critical questions to have in mind before hiring a pool cleaning service.

Let's dive in!

Why Should You Hire a Pool Cleaning Service in Las Vegas?

A pool is an investment like any other. Here are the reasons why you should hire a professional company to maintain and service your pool:

Regular Maintenance

For your pool to function optimally, you need to have a cleaning routine.

Setting routines can be hectic, but a company will take care of the process for you.

When you hire a professional pool service provider, they will:

  • Handle weekly cleaning for you
  • Offer high-quality pool maintenance services

It Saves Time and Money

While cleaning the pool by yourself might save you money, it's not always the case.

Unless you're a professional cleaner, there are a lot of things that might go wrong. When you hire trained technicians, they can easily identify the smallest errors earlier before they escalate.

For example, if a tiny crack is left unchecked, it might resurface your entire pool leading to accidents.

A company saves you money by extending the life of your pool equipment through regular check-ups and maintenance.

Peace of Mind

When you hire a professional company, you award yourself everlasting independence. You no longer have to worry about pump maintenance, levels of chemicals, and pool cleanup.

Since your pool is an area for fun and relaxation, you deserve a certified company to do the job for you.

Is it expensive?

However, keep in mind that the size and shape of your swimming pool might affect the overall cost.

Is the Company Certified?

You need to be sure the service providers are registered and licensed.

Each state has its minimum qualifications on who can service your pool and use the chemicals.

Some states are yet to establish the minimum qualifications to qualify as a pool cleaner.

If you fail to vet carefully, mistakes committed by novice cleaners might destroy your pool equipment.

At Clean Living Pool and Spa, we are proud to have a trained and licensed pool cleaning team.

Our company is registered by the National Swimming Pool Foundation (NSPF). Therefore, you can rely on our pool professionals since they have a better understanding of pool care.

Is the Company Experienced?

A company with years of experience in pool cleaning is more likely to perform a great job than a startup.

Many years in the field means experienced technicians. They understand the safety and health regulations to be followed when handling your pool.

In addition, they can give you great insights on how to get the most from your investment.

At Clean Living Home and Spa, we have over 10 years of experience cleaning residential and commercial pools in Las Vegas, NV.

We are also a customer-driven company that is committed to ensuring you get the best from your pool.

Does the Company Have Customer Reviews?

Pool cleaners in Las Vegas must always provide exemplary service to their clients. Check customer reviews online and even ask for referrals.

At Clean Living Pool and Spa, we are proud our customers have found our services outstanding.

As a family-owned company, we understand the need to have a clean pool for your family and business.

Does the Company Offer Repair Services?

Do the pool cleaners offer pool repair services? When your pool is cleaned by trained technicians, they can identify minimal defects like cracks before they worsen.

Early identification of defects prevents them from getting worse. To save your time and money, choose a provider with both cleaning and repair services.

That way, you save yourself the hassle of paying a different company to maintain and repair your pool.

What is the Service Area of the Company?

Does the Company Specialize in Residential or Commercial Pool Cleaning Services?

posts.excerpt: Pool consulting services are essential for Las Vegas pool owners to ensure proper water chemistry, maintenance, and emergency repairs. Professional service providers offer expertise, specialized equipment, and reliable support year-round.
posts.body:
Why Pool Consulting Services Are Crucial for Las Vegas Pool Owners

Selecting a pool service provider is essential in ensuring that your pool is constantly in the best condition. You also ought to evaluate several reasons before employing a swimming pool service provider, and doing so will benefit you in various ways.

You may need a service provider for the following seven reasons we have listed.

These include:

  1. They know the requirements of using chemicals
  2. For necessary pool maintenance
  3. For quick swimming pool services during a crisis
  4. Around the clock availability of pool service equipment
  5. Standardised swimming pool repair services
  6. The sheer experience of a pool service provider
  7. They provide reasonable pricing and value in the long run

They know the requirements of using chemicals.

The central part of your swimming pool comprises the water quality. The water quality per litre deduces the health and condition of your swimming pool.

Any duty provider you agree to hire must fully comprehend what is required to make the water fit for use. These service providers usually have a detailed understanding of the number of chemicals needed to decontaminate the water in the pool.

They also know about chlorination, the degree of alkalinity favourable for the human body, and the reasonable way to disinfect water safely.

For necessary pool maintenance

No matter how extraordinary the state of your swimming pool now is, what will ultimately determine its future is the necessary ongoing pool maintenance done by the service providers. The behavior they show when reacting to damage and performing repairs will deduce how long your pool will endure.

If they quickly repair parts, then you are safe. On the other hand, if they postpone the problems by allowing them to accumulate and worsen before solving them, this is a reminder of lousy maintenance culture and will harm your swimming pool in the long run.

They should have a faculty base or a team of workers that supervises the various aspects of swimming pool maintenance. They should also be quick in digging into issues that need to be rectified.

When all these necessary measures are put into place by the service provider, you can have peace of mind by knowing that your pool is in capable hands.

For quick swimming pool services during a crisis

Another aspect you should assess before finally endorsing a swimming pool service provider is their availability for quick swimming pool services.

Some service providers don't operate at certain times of the year due to the tools they possess or certain weather occurrences. Nonetheless, having a service provider ensure that your pool will be maintained at all costs is pertinent.

Some can only oversee your swimming pool during weekends, while some are ready to repair pools only during weekdays. Whatever time a swimming pool service provider wants to operate, be sure that it is a time that coincides with your timetable.

Nevertheless, it is vital to point out that some procedures can't be done in your pool at particular times of the year. Hence, your service provider should be available to make the most of the season when such procedures can be carried out without any hassle.

Another critical factor is the area. How quickly can your swimming pool service team get to your house if there is a crisis? If a contractor is close by, he can provide services immediately.

Around the clock availability of pool service equipment

The accessible equipment that a swimming pool contractor has is also a crucial thing to consider. Your swimming pool provider can only provide services based on the equipment ready for immediate use.

Swimming pools require specialised devices that work for water treatments, cleansing, and taking care of the habitat. All these things are important, and you need to make sure that the service provider you're looking to hire can provide these services.

Standardized swimming pool repair service

Over time, the service provider that you employ will come to see that your swimming pool requires repairs during regular maintenance. To be prepared to fulfil this, they need a reliable repair service that can troubleshoot the reason for each problem they will encounter while conserving your pool.

The efficacy of this restoration service will conclude how fast the dangers will be recognized and solved before these even have the chance to worsen. If the service provider cannot efficiently and effectively deliver this, you may need to consider employing a new pool service.

The experience level of the Pool Service Provider

The experience of a swimming pool assistance provider should be one of the essential things to assess Their level of understanding, most times, equates to their trustworthy skills and services.

The main reason you need an assistance provider is that you don't have any skilled expertise in sustaining a pool and most service providers are proficient at confronting any challenge that may occur.

Do not assume that a service provider has the vital expertise you need. Instead, explore their work experience.

Find out where they have operated previously and see if you can get in touch with the people where they formerly provided their services. Try to discover what other people are saying about them on service reviews.

Go online, look for inquiries, and search for suggestions about their services. Also, don't ignore asking about their license because it ensures professionalism.

Don't commit the error of assuming they have the necessary licenses without checking first.

Also, inquire if they have any regulating bodies or institutes that can hold them accountable in any problems.

They provide reasonable pricing and value in the long run

The final thing and, maybe, the most significant one to consider is a reasonable price in the long run.

It is not sufficient to only have a swimming pool service provider that is very helpful and useful, but the costs implicated should also fall within your expectations.

You want a service provider that you can afford. So, make sure that you compare the rate lists of different providers and go for one that you can afford.

They indeed provide valuable and practical tools and measures to keep your pool up to date.

posts.title: How to Drain A Swimming Pool - 5 Easy Ways
posts.author: Clean Living Pool and Spa
posts.alt_text: How to Drain A Swimming Pool - 5 Easy Ways
posts.excerpt: A comprehensive guide explaining why and when to drain your pool, plus five simple steps to safely and effectively drain it yourself using a submersible pump.
posts.body:
Step-by-Step Guide on How to Drain a Swimming Pool

Are you looking for cost effective and efficient ways to drain your pool?

Well, you have come to the right place.

The summer season calls for a refreshing swim, and what better way to start than with a newly filled pool?

Why drain your pool?

Draining a pool can be a tedious, expensive task. But, you have to do it at least every 2-3 years to ensure your water is clean.

And there are numerous reasons why it's mandatory and should be done.

The reasons include:

  • For resetting the water:

    In cases where your pool's PH has been seriously imbalanced, you can consider the option of resetting the water.

    The only way to maintain your pool's chemical environment is by having an optimal circulation. But in time, you may need to drain the water to reset it.

  • For pool maintenance:

    This is especially important if you need to fix a cracked tile at the bottom or for some necessary maintenance. Although you can maintain a pool while still having water in it, draining the swimming pool can help you ensure no problem is left behind.

When to Drain Your Pool

It is equally significant to know when to drain your pool. After you have concluded why you want to go ahead with it, figure out when to get the process started.

This could be when:

  • You are ready to pay for it:

    Draining a pool can be costly - at the minimum, you'll need to replenish a large volume of water. So, when you're sure that you have got the bills covered, start the task.

  • You have got plenty of time:

    The process doubtlessly requires an ample amount of time.

    The draining process can last anywhere from eight to sixteen hours alone and of course the filling process can require more time.

    It's essential that you do your research and manage time effectively before you commence the task.

  • It's bright and sunny

    You want to drain your pool on a sunny day. If it's raining, you're highly unlikely to get the work done.

    So, watch out for the weather and look up the forecast a day or two before.

  • The chemical levels have settled:

    Once the pool's chemical levels are down and the PH is neutral, we advise you to start draining the pool. You want to make sure that there aren't too many chemicals.

  • The circulation system is prepped:

    You want to turn off the automatic timer to ensure your pump does not turn on to let air in during the process.

Now, If you think you are ready to drain your pool after reading the above reasons and conditions, then check out these simple yet effective ways.

They will guide you to complete the task in no time.

We have listed the steps in the right order making it even more feasible for you to follow.

These include:

  1. Installing a submersible pump
  2. Draining the pool slowly
  3. Removing the pump
  4. Relieving the valves
  5. Performing any necessary maintenance

Start today by following these 5 simple steps and drain your pool as safely as possible. So, let's dive in and take a closer look at the tasks to be done.

Installing a submersible pump

The regular pump that you use will definitely get you going in draining the water. But, as soon as the water is removed from the pool, it will start collecting air leading to permanent damage.

Save yourself the hassle and rent a submersible pump to get the job done. That way you would not have to pay extra and will be able to get down to work in no time.

Start by pacing the submersible pump at the center of the pool and connect it to a long hose. The other end of the hose will be connected to the place where you want to drain the water by providing an outlet for the water to flow.

Remember to use a long power cord too that reaches right down to the bottom of the pool.

Draining the pool slowly

This is the most vital part of the process - in fact, it is your goal.

Turn on the pump and start draining slowly. It is important that you are present at the site while the process takes place so that you may take notice of the cords and hoses at all times.

Likewise, remember to switch onto salt water so that the chlorine in the pool is at the lowest level. You can achieve this through a salt water generator which is extremely useful in such situations.

Removing the pump

When you reach a level when the pump will no longer be shifting water out of the pool that is the time you want to remove the pump.

You should not ideally remove every possible inch of water out of the pool as it can disrupt the chemical balance of the pool.

When you have achieved this, move onto the next step.

Relieving the valves

When you're done removing the pump, it's time to relieve hydrostatic pressure on the underground water.

You can do so by relieving the valves.

Perform any necessary maintenance

You may have drained the pool for chemically balancing it for future use or any necessary maintenance. If you need to fix any cracks or paint the pool proceed to doing it now and follow that with refilling the pool within a week.

Painting a pool does not take much energy. However, it takes time and can seldom turn out to cost you a lot.

Now that we have done explaining, perform the above steps to get the desired outcome. When you are done with everything, take a plunge into that pool to relax and swim at your ease.

Drain your pool conveniently and quickly.

If you need to get your pool drained and repaired, Clean Living Pool and Spa has got your needs covered.

As a premium service provider, our mission is to provide quality repair services at a cost-friendly budget.

Clean Living Pool and Spa's services start at a affordable price of just $75.

Our services include:

  • Motor Repair / Cleaning / Replacement
  • Pool Vacuum Installation/Replacement
  • Pump Repair/Replacement

So, visit our website for more details here or call (702) 539-3927

We'll ensure that everything is back to the way it's supposed to be.

Contact us today for great services!
posts.description: A comprehensive guide explaining why and when to drain your pool, plus five simple steps to safely and effectively drain it yourself using a submersible pump.

posts.title: Pool Care: A Comprehensive Guide to Take Care of A Pool (8 Easy Ways)
posts.author: Clean Living Pool and Spa
posts.alt_text: Pool Care: A Comprehensive Guide to Take Care of A Pool (8 Easy Ways)
posts.excerpt: Regular pool maintenance requires weekly cleaning and attention to detail. Follow these eight simple steps to keep your pool safe, clean, and enjoyable throughout the season.
posts.body:
The swimming pool is a popular place during the summer and is enjoyed by adults and children alike.

Regular visits require weekly maintenance and cleaning. So, follow these simple steps to keep your pool at its best this season.

  1. Figure out how to chlorinate water
  2. Buy equipment, chemicals, and cleaners
  3. Install a pool cleaner and clean filters regularly
  4. Maintain the pool's PH levels
  5. Remember to shock your pool
  6. Check the baskets
  7. Skim the surface and brush edges
  8. Keep a clean pool deck

Taking care of a pool takes time

Some common problems that may arise include:

  • Clogging of pool filters due to debris from surrounding trees and bushes.
  • Poor pool circulation because of clogged jets, underpowered pumps, and inefficient filters.
  • Alkalinity as a result of unbalanced PH.
  • Bacterial and fungal growth from long periods of hot, sunny weather.

Therefore, it is important to maintain your pool using cost-effective methods so that you may enjoy this amenity at its best.

You may be inclined to opt for professional services to get the task done.

However, if you have the right equipment and know-how to follow some simple techniques, you can maintain your pool like a pro.

So let's take a look at how to overcome some of these common problems and take care of your pool in some easy steps.

No. 1: Figure out how to chlorinate water

Chlorinating the pool water is essentially vital and there are potentially numerous ways to administer chlorine. You can invest in a high-end saltwater system that produces chlorine on its own or do it manually yourself.

Each method has its own pros and cons.

For example:

  • If you opt for a saltwater system, you will avoid the hassle of doing a lot of things mentioned in the article as it uses safer chlorine levels and requires less maintenance.
  • However, these systems are expensive and not cost-friendly like regular chlorine.

No. 2: Buy equipment, chemicals, and cleaners

Firstly, you need suitable test equipment to check the PH, alkalinity, calcium hardness, and chlorine. The kit contains a series of chemicals that respond to the other added chemicals in the pool and a container.

The test equipment ensures that your pool is safe to use.

Secondly, you need to visit a pool supply store and purchase pool chemicals such as calcium, chlorine, algaecides, and stabilizers.

It is advisable to talk to a specialist at the store who can guide you on how to safely use these chemicals.

Additionally, you must purchase a cleaner. These usually come in three different types and you can determine which one suits you best:

  • Pressure side cleaners which relieve pressure on the filter. The only drawback is they might require an extra booster.
  • Robotic cleaners that clean effectively but are much more expensive than regular cleaners.
  • Suction-side pool cleaners which are low in cost and easy to use. The only problem with these cleaners is that they increase pressure on the filter and require a pump.

No. 3: Install pool cleaners and clean filters regularly

It is advisable to connect your pool cleaner weekly. This will aid in reducing the number of chemicals you need to add to maintain your pool.

Depending on the type of filter you are using (sand, cartridge, and diatomaceous), you need to use different techniques to clean each type.

Remember to turn off the system and bleed the air out before starting.

  • For a sand filter detach the filter from the pool system's plumbing and do it at the very beginning of the season.
  • Cartridge filters need to be removed from the base which will allow you to spray down the filter with a hose.
  • Diatomaceous filters need to be backwashed and sprayed once a month.

No. 4: Maintain the pool's PH

Test your pool for PH, alkalinity, and calcium hardness using a chemical tester kit once a week.

The Color-coded guide on the test kit will help you to determine the number of chemicals required.

Doing so creates a safe environment for everyone to enjoy.

No. 5: Remember to shock the pool

This step should be done weekly and it is significant for maintaining PH levels and keeping algae at bay. A basic shock or a multifunctional shock system can be used here. The only difference between the two is that a multifunctional system gets the job done faster and stabilizes the chemicals.

No. 6: Check the baskets

Basket filters collect small and large debris and every pool has at least one of these. This is where decomposing leaves and other debris usually end up and may harm your pool's safety. You can use a hose to spray the basket after detaching it from the base.

No. 7: Skim the surfaces and brush edges

It may seem like a burdensome task to do every day but in reality, it can save you a lot of trouble in the long run. If avoided for several days, it can lead to the growth of bacteria or algae and make the pool unsightly.

No. 8: Keep a clean pool deck

Develop a habit to clean the pool's sides daily with a brush. The dirt that comes off from the edges can be directed to a filter.

If the pool's deck is dirty, know that the dirt will eventually end up in the pool. So, take your time to sweep the surface clean as needed.

Now you can take care of your pool easily

Most people enjoy swimming at home or in public pools in hot weather to beat the heat. So it is pivotal to maintain the pool's cleanliness and safety for everyone.

You can make the most of this luxury by simply abiding by some easy steps. Even if you are a novice or an expert, this article will have hopefully covered all the essential steps required.

These 8 steps will get you in the pool right away!
posts.description: Regular pool maintenance requires weekly cleaning and attention to detail. Follow these eight simple steps to keep your pool safe, clean, and enjoyable throughout the season.

posts.title: Is Hiring a Swimming Pool Maintenance Service Worth It?

posts.alt_text: Is Hiring a Swimming Pool Maintenance Service Worth It?
posts.excerpt: Professional pool maintenance services offer expertise, proper equipment, and preventive care that make pool ownership more convenient and ensure longer-lasting, safer pools for your family.
posts.body:
Is Hiring a Pool Maintenance Service Worth the Investment?

Owning a swimming pool gives you something for you and your friends and family to enjoy. But as for anything you may own and regularly use, it requires maintenance.

Maintaining a pool requires more than simply cleaning or replacing the water regularly. There can be mechanical components that you might need to consider and look after.

So pool maintenance can also be highly technical, especially if you have features like filters, temperature regulators, lighting and others.

If you have the time and expertise to implement regular inspection, cleaning and repairing to ensure a safe pool to use, you can save a little bit of money by going DIY.

But like most pool owners, you might be too busy to do things yourself.

And the good news is that you can guarantee an excellent, clean, safe and a properly functioning pool without stepping out of your own schedule to do all the maintenance work.

You can simply hire a professional contractor to make it easy for you.

And is it worth it?

Yes.

Here are 7 reasons why hiring a swimming pool maintenance contractor is worth your money

  1. They have the proper tools for any need.
  2. They are expert troubleshooters.
  3. They can do maintenance work quicker and safer.
  4. They offer preventive maintenance inspections and interventions.
  5. They are up to date with the latest technology.
  6. They can ensure that your pool passes any regulations.
  7. They can keep your pool in excellent condition for longer periods.

They have the proper tools for any need

Most homeowners who own a pool, don't have the proper tools for perfect cleaning and repair.

But since a pool maintenance service is specialized to ensure clean and functioning pools, they surely would have all the equipment needed to do the job.

If you go DIY, you may quickly find out that you need to buy a certain list of equipment to do the job. So it is more convenient for you to simply call a contractor - someone who already has whatever your pool may need.

They are expert troubleshooters

Pool maintenance service providers already have extensive experience in repairing, maintaining and even improving pools. So you can be sure that they would know what they're doing when you call them for any pool problem you have.

When you go DIY, there might be issues that you've never encountered before. And to address it you may have to hire a professional anyway.

You may not have the time to figure all of these out but if you do, you still won't be able to fix things as fast as a professional can do.

Also, when you resort to trial and error, the chance of "error" can possibly worsen whatever problem you're trying to handle. So it still would be best to simply hand the work to someone who can quickly identify your pool problem and implement that exact, effective solution.

They can do maintenance work quicker and safer.

A professional swimming pool maintenance service provider has the expertise to quickly assess your pool for problems and improvement needs. Unlike you who may have to guess and double-check on articles on the internet, they know pools like the back of their hand.

They have expert eyes on the signs and symptoms of a problem and can give you excellent advice based on their evaluation.

Also, when they work, they ensure complete safety. They are trained and have had to pass required certifications as a professional contractor.

They offer preventive maintenance, inspections and interventions.

While you may be able to perform your swimming pool cleaning as it can be a simple process, other maintenance-related requirements can involve more than simply ensuring fresh, germ-free water.

Surely, for example, you know the right amount of chlorine to add when you replace the water in your pool. But there's more to swimming pool maintenance than just cleaning it.

You also need to do preventive inspections to see if a component of your pool is still okay to use. And when you find anything that is close to its expiry or predict that a component might need replacement soon, you should be able to take the appropriate action.

And you can do that on your own given the correct expertise but most private pool owners won't even have the time to do so.

And of course, nothing beats a professional's expert eyes and solutions.

A professional swimming pool maintenance service will inspect your pool and all of its components holistically, ensuring that nothing is left to chance.

Remember that one active wire left open or a slippery area can cause a severe accident for your pool users.

You don't want that. And you can avoid these problems with a professional doing expert maintenance work on your pool.

They are up to date with the latest technology.

Day by day, technology improves the way we enjoy things, keep things safe and reduce costs.

When you consult with a professional swimming pool maintenance service provider, they will be able to offer you the latest technology that you may want to employ. And these new tools and components can improve the safety, features and energy efficiency of your pool.

They can ensure that your pool meets local ordinances

Owning a pool can be like owning a car. And surely there can be government-imposed regulations you may need to pass and continue adhering to as a pool owner.

The government usually puts these regulations in place to ensure the safety of your home or property and the houses and people around you.

But having to keep up with these standards can be a hassle to do on your own.

With a professional swimming pool maintenance service, you can ensure that your pool passes any requirement that enables you to own and use it every time.

They can keep your pool in excellent condition for longer

As for anything you may own, proper maintenance of your swimming pool can help you enjoy it for a longer time. And the best people to do that are experts.

When you hire a professional swimming pool maintenance service, you can ensure that your pool and its components won't quickly wear and tear. And you can enjoy a safe and clean swimming pool that will last you for decades.

Hire an Expert Swimming Pool Maintenance Service Today

If you are looking to make your life as a private pool owner easy and convenient, and want to ensure a safe, enjoyable and excellent pool for you and your family and friends to use every time, you need an expert to look after it for you.

And the best for the job is our company, Clean Living Pool and Spa. We are experts in pool maintenance, repairs, preventive solutions and upgrades.

We make it convenient for you to own a clean pool in your property.
posts.description: Professional pool maintenance services offer expertise, proper equipment, and preventive care that make pool ownership more convenient and ensure longer-lasting, safer pools for your family.

posts.excerpt: A comprehensive guide outlining eight essential steps for effectively repairing pool liners, from detecting leaks to applying patches. The article emphasizes proper preparation and technique to ensure lasting repairs.
posts.body:
Step-by-Step Guide to Repairing a Pool Liner

Sure enough, a swimming pool can bring so much enjoyment and happiness to you and your family. But, you also have to consider that, just as any other thing, it needs maintenance.

If you have a swimming pool in your home, one of the things that you should consider is tearing of the pool liner.

Here are 8 ways on how to repair a pool liner:

  • Make sure there is a leak.
  • Clean the area.
  • Choose the patch suitable for the size of your leak.
  • Prep your patch.
  • Apply the patch adhesive.
  • Stick the patch to the leak.
  • Hold the patch in place.
  • Double patch if necessary.

How To Repair A Pool Liner

Tip No. 1: Make sure there is a leak

Simply because your pool is losing water does not always indicate that there is a leak.

You have to remember that most pools lose between 3 and 5 inches of water each day due to evaporation throughout the summer. To sum it up, that equates to around 25,000-50,000 gallons of water each year!

So before you determine that you really must patch your pool liner, verify if there is a leak to repair and find it.

As a standard guide, if you're losing an inch or more of water every day, that means you've got a leak.

There are many ways to determine this. Here are two of those methods:

Bucket method

To do this, just put a five-gallon bucket filled with water near your pool. Then, after marking the water line in your bucket and pool (while turning off the pump), leave it alone for 24 hours.

After 24 hours, if the water level in your pool is lower than the water level in your bucket, you most likely have a leak.

Ink method

This method assumes you are quite certain you have a leak and have a basic sense of where it is.

You'll need a leak-finding kit or some dark food coloring to do this test. Then, while wearing goggles, inject a little amount of color in the region where you believe the leak is.

If there is a leak, the dye will flow gradually toward it, flowing out with the rest of the water.

Tip No. 2: Clean the area.

After making sure that you have a leak, clean the area and prepare it for patching up.

Any trash, algae, sunscreen, or other material attached to your pool walls makes it more difficult for a patch or adhesive to attach.

So gently clean the vinyl surrounding the hole with a non-abrasive scouring pad.

Avoid scrubbing. This may make the hole bigger, and you wouldn't want that to happen.

Caution: Unless absolutely required, we highly advise against emptying your pool. If you find yourself in need of draining your pool for whatever reason, contact a professional for assistance.

Remember that most of the repairs must be done underwater since draining your pool may allow it to collapse or cause more liner damage.

Additionally, sunshine exposure may cause a dry liner to become brittle and inefficient.

Tip No. 3: Choose the patch suitable for the size of your leak

Patches come in a variety of styles, including peel-and-stick, vinyl kits, and patch material. The kind you choose will be determined by the leak's location and your own preference.

The sort of patch you need depends on the location and extent of the leak.

If the leak isn't too big, waterproof tape or a peel-and-stick repair will suffice. If the rip is bigger, you'll need a decent patch kit.

Tip No. 4: Prep your patch.

Whichever method of patching you use, be careful to prepare it first. This involves reducing it to the right size or topping it with your waterproof tape to create a patch that properly covers the area.

Ensure that the patch is at least several inches bigger than the hole or tear that caused the leak.

Tip No. 5: Apply the patch adhesive

After making sure your patch is prepped, it's now time to apply your adhesive. If it is not included in your patch kit, you may go the extra mile by buying one.

Ensure that the glue is applied to the back of the patch, not the lining.

Tip No. 6: Stick the patch to the leak

In this step, you have to make sure that the patch sticks to the leak smoothly. Level out any seams and gently push out any bubbles, if there are any, with your fingers.

Tip No. 7: Hold the patch in place.

Hold the patch firmly in place for a few minutes to allow the glue to set. Apply pressure for 24 hours if necessary.

This may be achieved by placing a weighted item on it if it is at the bottom of the pool, or by utilizing a clamp of some kind on the pool wall.

Tip No. 8: Double patch if necessary.

Placing a second patch over the first will not strengthen your repair, but if it makes you feel more secure, then you can double up.

To do this, make sure the first patch has fully dried before applying the second.

Cut the second patch at least two inches wider in diameter than the first patch (four inches larger than the hole) and repeat the same process as you did with the first patch.

Make Your Pool Liner Repair More Efficient

With all the steps mentioned above, you might also consider these to make your repair more effective:

Prep your things ahead of time.

Before you begin, place all of your tools alongside the pool. It's difficult to get in and out of the pool to grab what you need.

Work quickly.

While the glue is waterproof, the less it comes into touch with water, the better.

It adheres better if you can get it to the surface rapidly and put it on.

One of the things you can do is to fold the patch in half with the adhesive on the inside. Then, immerse it in water and unfold it just before sticking it to the leak.

Call the experts

Your patchwork, no matter how well done, may eventually fall apart.

Furthermore, you can only repair the same holes so many times before they become larger and larger.

It's not your fault; it's simply part of owning a pool.

In cases when simple patching doesn't do the job, you might want to consider calling the pool professionals.

Clean Living Pool and Spa is committed to delivering the highest-quality pool repair service in Las Vegas. Aside from pool repair, they also do pool maintenance and consulting services.

So, if you notice that your pool liner is tearing at the seams and patching up won't fix it, Clean Living Pool and Spa would be very happy to assist you.

posts.body:
The Importance of Weekly Pool Cleaning in Las Vegas

When you own a pool in Las Vegas, you have an investment that can help you earn influence if not money.

People go to Vegas for fun, and if you have a facility that they can use for parties, events and celebrations, you will constantly have guests, if not bookings with clients.

So if you own a pool in your home or venue, you will need to keep things clean to keep things going.

Here are 7 reasons why you need weekly pool cleaning service in Las Vegas.

  • You always want an attractive pool.
  • A dirty pool can cause sickness and accidents.
  • Weekly pool maintenance can make you money.
  • Weekly pool maintenance can save you money.
  • Proper focus on upkeep can prevent legal problems.
  • It's difficult to do DIY pool cleaning.
  • Professional cleaning makes things convenient and effective.

Reason No. 1: You always want an attractive pool.

You're in Vegas. So you want to make your property fabulous for your family and guests.

And that includes your pool.

If you host parties and events in your facility, you want to impress guests or clients with a sparkling pool. You can only keep your pool looking its best with quality cleaning work and maintenance on the regular.

And obviously, nobody wants to book or use a pool that looks dirty and unkempt. So your pool has to be clean and look clean.

With weekly maintenance service, your pool can always look as good as new while being clean and spot-free. And your guests or clients will love it, so they will keep on coming back for a good time.

Reason No. 2: A dirty pool can cause sickness

Users of your pool can come from anywhere, and they naturally bring microorganisms with them. So if you leave your pool unmaintained and uncleaned, it can collect dirt that attracts and breeds bacteria and viruses.

And a significant amount of these germs in your pool can cause sickness to you and everyone else who uses the facility.

So if your pool is used regularly by guests or clients, you also need to clean it regularly to get rid of dangerous microbes. And a pool maintenance service cleaning your pool weekly can do the job.

They have the proper and effective disinfection tools and methods that will keep your pool germ-free.

On the other hand, bacteria and algae in your pool can cause slippery and slimy spots. And these can be dangerous to your pool users because one slip can be dangerous.

So you need a weekly pool cleaning service to eliminate these slippery spots and keep your facility free from accidents.

Reason No. 3: Weekly pool maintenance can make you money.

When people know that you do weekly maintenance for your pool, they will highly recommend your facility for events and parties.

Clients are always looking for quality venues for their celebrations, and a beautiful pool makes a great first impression.

So if you are to attract event organizers always who consider your pool for their Vegas event, you want them to know that you actively keep the facility clean.

Some will even want to know who maintains your pool to see whether you get quality cleaning.

A good reputation of a clean pool facility can keep clients coming in. And you can even get VIP events that pay big when A-list organizers know your venue to be a reliable and clean place to party.

Reason No. 4: Weekly pool maintenance can save you money.

Indeed, leaving your pool uncleaned even for a week can cause bacteria, algae, grime, and more to collect. Especially in the Vegas heat...regular maintenance is important to keep things looking good.

Also, when the dirt in your pool stacks up because of lack of maintenance, the cleaning work you need to do after will be massive.

All of these results of not regularly cleaning your pool can be expensive.

Cleaning a severely dirty pool can cost more money. And having to fix damage caused by dirt can require costly repairs.

But when you have your pool maintained, cleaned, and checked for potential damages weekly, you save money. A pool cleaning service can keep things clean and prevent damages yet fix them as early as when they come up and still aren't severe.

Reason No. 5: Proper focus on upkeep can prevent legal problems.

If someone who recently used your pool becomes sick, you don't want your facility to be the reason why. If a guest has become ill because of using your dirty pool, you might get sued.

So you want to guarantee that your pool doesn't cause any disease to anyone. And you can ensure this by having your pool weekly maintained and making it clear to your clients that you do so with professional service.

The worst thing about a dirty pool is its potential to cause accidents because of slippery parts. And a small trip or slip in your facility can cause a large litigation, especially if it's a VIP mishap.

Reason No. 6: It's difficult to do DIY pool cleaning.

And, yes, pool cleaning takes time that you might not have and expertise that you can't acquire overnight.

If you do your own pool maintenance, who's going to manage your events? You need someone to do the job for you, and you need them to do it right.

If you do pool cleaning on your own, you need to be prepared to invest the time and effort to do it right. And without the proper tools and methods, it can take plenty of manual effort to ensure a clean pool.

With a professional doing things for you, your pool will be cleaned fast and thoroughly.

So you don't have to do things yourself. Simply work smart and get a pool maintenance service.

Reason No. 7: Professional cleaning makes things convenient and effective.

With a professional pool cleaner doing the job for you, you can guarantee that your facility is always ready to use.

A professional pool maintenance provider has the tools and methods that make for fast and thorough cleaning. And done weekly, you can prevent any damages and problems caused by dirt from happening to your pool.

Getting a weekly pool maintenance service is suitable for any pool, whether it is for private use or offered to clients.

Get your pool cleaned the smart and easy way.

If you are looking for someone to keep things good as new for you or your customers, you need a reliable pool maintenance service.

Clean Living Pool and Spa provides weekly pool cleaning services in Las Vegas for any size of pool.

We make things easy and convenient for you to enjoy a dirt-free pool everybody wants to use.
